# Simran Shrestha — Portfolio

> Personal portfolio site for Simran Shrestha, a Computer Science senior at Northern Kentucky University
> specializing in data engineering, CI/CD automation, and backend development.

## Contact

## Education
**Northern Kentucky University** — Highland Heights, KY
Bachelor of Science in Computer Science | Minor in Data Science
Expected Graduation: May 2026

## Experience

### Data Automation Intern — Great American Insurance Group (Sep 2025 – Present)
- Developed an integration testing system for an Informatica CI/CD pipeline, creating a scalable framework for early issue detection.
- Analyzed GitHub CI workflows performing SAST scans to identify and remediate critical and high CVEs.
- Built a Postman collection for Snowflake integration featuring automated JWT generation, UDF/stored procedure queries, and Snowflake SQL API execution.
- Accelerated FastAPI application deployment by building a centralized repository following team best practices.
- Documented a previously undocumented repository including setup, local testing, and contribution guidelines.
- Developed unit tests for an AI application that builds business dossiers from public resources using Tavily and Azure Maps.
- Produced comprehensive Copilot usage guidelines using best practices from within the organization and the broader development community.
- Authored Copilot code-review guidelines to enforce Python coding standards, improve code quality, and reduce errors across the org.
- Organized interviews with 25 members across the enterprise spanning data, AI automation, business, and security teams.
- Collaborated in an Agile environment with a multidisciplinary team.

### Data Engineer Intern — Great American Insurance Group (May 2024 – Aug 2024)
- Used Informatica Cloud (IDMC), SQL, and Snowflake to troubleshoot and validate financial datasets for month-end reporting.
- Authored documentation for Informatica jobs, streamlining knowledge transfer for support teams.
- Built and maintained SharePoint Modern sites to organize SOPs and improve visibility of team processes.

### Teaching Assistant — Introduction to Python, Northern Kentucky University (Aug 2023 – Dec 2023)
- Tutored 22 students through weekly office hours and problem-solving sessions.
- Led live coding demonstrations to build stronger debugging and problem-solving mindsets.

## Projects

### NKU AI Chatbot
Stack: Python, BeautifulSoup, GPT-2, Jina API
Scraped and cleaned 1,611 data samples from NKU's website, converted them to text files, and fine-tuned GPT-2.
Achieved 10–15% better response accuracy on NKU-related queries compared to ChatGPT.

## Skills
Python, SQL, Java, R, Git, GitHub Actions, Docker, CI/CD, Snowflake, Informatica Cloud (IDMC), FastAPI, Postman, Microsoft 365
